title: Chapter 3 - IP Addresses and DNS
description: Check if you understand what IP addresses are and what DNS does.

questions.prompt: What is the main purpose of an IP address?

options.label: To identify where data should be delivered on a network

explanations.overview: IP addresses are used for addressing and routing on networks.

byOptionId.d: Correct. It’s the address used to deliver network traffic.
byOptionId.a: Encryption is not the job of IP addresses.
byOptionId.c: HTML is for page structure, not networking.

questions.prompt: What does DNS do when you type `example.com` in your browser?

options.label: Turns an IP address into a CSS file
Turns a domain name into an IP address
Forces the website to load faster

explanations.overview: DNS helps browsers find the IP address for a domain name so they can connect to the right server.

byOptionId.b: Correct. DNS translates names to addresses.
byOptionId.d: DNS doesn’t force speed; it’s just part of the lookup process.
byOptionId.c: Web servers create content, not DNS.

questions.prompt: Which one looks like an IPv4 address?

explanations.overview: IPv4 addresses use four dot-separated numbers.

byOptionId.a: Correct. Four numbers separated by dots is typical IPv4 format.
byOptionId.d: That’s a URL, not an IP address.
byOptionId.b: That’s a URL fragment, not an IP address.

questions.prompt: In DNS, what does TTL (time to live) control?

options.label: How fast the website will load in the browser
How long a resolver may cache a DNS answer (in seconds)
The maximum size of DNS responses
The number of DNS servers for a domain

explanations.overview: TTL tells caching resolvers how long they can keep a DNS record before asking again.

byOptionId.b: Correct — TTL is measured in seconds and controls caching time.
byOptionId.a: Load speed is affected by many things; TTL is about caching DNS answers.
byOptionId.c: TTL is not about response size.
byOptionId.d: The number of nameservers is unrelated to TTL.
